name: Preset Managed Apache Superset API
what_it_does: Managed Apache Superset cloud service with REST API — enabling data teams to deploy Apache Superset without infrastructure management, with enterprise features including SSO, workspace isolation, embedded analytics for customer-facing dashboards, and Superset-native API for chart, dashboard, and dataset management.
use_cases: Programmatically creating and managing Superset charts and dashboards via Preset API for analytics automation
Embedding Preset/Superset dashboards in customer-facing applications via Guest Token API
Managing Superset datasets and database connections via Preset API for data pipeline integration
Administering workspace users and roles via Preset API for enterprise user provisioning
Querying Preset workspace usage and dashboard activity via API for analytics governance
not_for: Non-technical self-service BI (Tableau or Power BI are more user-friendly for business users)
Real-time operational monitoring (use Grafana for metrics and alerting dashboards)
Organizations without SQL-comfortable data teams (Superset requires SQL knowledge)
best_when: A data engineering team wants managed Apache Superset without DevOps overhead — especially for embedding analytics in customer-facing products or building data products where the open-source Superset API is well-understood.
avoid_when: You don't use or want Apache Superset, need business-user-friendly BI without SQL, or have a small team where the Superset complexity isn't justified.

auth.notes: Service Account token (API key) for workspace-level access. OAuth2 for user-delegated access. Bearer token auth. Workspace-scoped access. No webhooks. Guest Token API for embedded analytics embedding without user login. REST API is Apache Superset API (open standard). SAML/SCIM SSO for enterprise identity. Preset Manager API for workspace administration (separate from Superset API).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 5 users, limited workspace features
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $50/month (10 users); Professional $500/month (25 users); Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2020. Raised $4.6M seed (2021). Backed by a16z, Altimeter Capital. Founded by Max Beauchemin (original Apache Superset creator at Airbnb). Open-source Superset foundation makes Preset an extension of community investment. Embedded analytics (Guest Token) for product analytics use case is key differentiator vs. self-hosting. Vertica, BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, and 40+ database connectors. Growing adoption among data-native companies building analytics products.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per workspace; contact support for specifics

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API is standard Apache Superset API — well-documented open-source standard. Service account token auth. Good documentation leveraging Superset open-source community. Guest Token API for embedded use cases. No webhooks. Open-source Superset API documentation is extensive community resource. Preset Manager API (workspace admin) is separate and less documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Chart reads fully idempotent. Dashboard reads idempotent. Dataset reads idempotent. Chart creation requires check for existing chart by title. Dashboard creation not idempotent — check by title.
known_agent_gotchas: API is Apache Superset API — two separate APIs: Superset (content) and Preset Manager (admin)
Guest Token expires after 5 minutes — must refresh for embedded analytics sessions
US data residency only — EU customers need to evaluate GDPR adequacy
Open-source Superset API changes with Apache releases — verify version compatibility
Early-stage company (2020) — evaluate long-term viability and acquisition risk

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. Service account token + OAuth2. a16z-backed company with startup security maturity. Guest Token for embedded analytics must be generated server-side — never expose Service Account token client-side. Analytics data may include sensitive business metrics — configure row-level security in Superset. SAML/SCIM SSO for enterprise access control.
